D.meaning: Jaccard distance on misclassified solver sets; zero means identical sets

A.meaning: max over lambda and i of |cost_C(i,lambda) - cost_B1(i,lambda)|; zero means identical per-solver costs at every lambda

cost_function_inherited_from_c1.formula: cost(decision, ground_truth, lambda_R, lambda_A) = lambda_A if false_accept, lambda_R if false_reject, 0 if correct

aggregate_consistency_check.check: For each (estimator, population), recomputed (WA, WR) from per-solver decisions must equal saved (WA, WR) in data/midweather_fingerprint_lc{322,45}.json
aggregate_consistency_check.on_failure: STOP IMMEDIATELY. Do not record any result. Surface discrepancy to Foued. C-3a is not authorized to resolve silently.
aggregate_consistency_check.rationale: The aggregate is the only prior measurement. Inconsistency means the re-run protocol is wrong OR the aggregate was not produced by this solver pack. Both require Foued call.

three_case_decision_rule.interpretation: M_C == M_B1. C and B1 misclassify the same solvers. The aggregate tie is real, not a cancellation artifact.
M_C != M_B1, but per-solver costs are uniformly equal. Structural difference does not produce cost difference under the freeze's symmetric-within-type metric.
M_C != M_B1 AND per-solver costs differ. The only case where a per-solver-weighted cost functional (C-2 territory) could in principle exploit the difference. C-3a reports it; it does not run C-2.
